06 Operational Signals → Pain Mapping
Air Freight Urgency (31.4% imports)
HIGH probability
101 import shipments by air = reactive urgency pattern. OEM customer sensitivity to production stoppages drives air spend that should be avoidable with better supplier synchronization.
IMMEX / Virtual Complexity
HIGH probability
109 import shipments classified as unknown/virtual — IMMEX transfers, regime changes and regularization generate significant administrative and customs coordination burden.
Multi-Hub Fragmentation
HIGH probability
6 critical operational hubs (Colombia NL, Altamira, MTY, Puebla, AICM, Nuevo Laredo) with no unified visibility layer. Each hub likely managed by separate agents or carriers.
Spain + India Supplier Coordination
MEDIUM-HIGH
230 shipments from Spain and India combined. Multi-timezone, multi-currency supplier coordination with documentation requirements that increase error probability.
John Deere OEM Sensitivity
MEDIUM-HIGH
Strong OEM customer dependency elevates the cost of any delay, documentation error or customs hold. Production continuity is non-negotiable for OEM-tier suppliers.
Cross-Border Colombia / Laredo Dependency
MEDIUM-HIGH
~207 shipments through Colombia NL make this a structural dependency. Any friction at this crossing point impacts the entire export operation to USA.
07a AIOS Green Flags
  • ✓Alta recurrencia: 627 embarques totales — operación activa y constante.
  • ✓John Deere ecosystem: OEM dependency elevates every service to critical tier.
  • ✓Elevated air exposure: 101 import + 22 export air shipments = immediate entry opportunity.
  • ✓Real multimodality: Maritime, truck, air and virtual active simultaneously.
  • ✓Customs complexity: IMMEX, virtual transfers and multiple customs points = high value for Akzent brokerage.
  • ✓Diversified international sourcing: Spain, India, USA and Germany — multi-origin coordination complexity.
07b AIOS Red Flags
  • ⚠John Deere concentration: Single OEM dependency = single point of commercial risk.
  • ⚠High unknown/virtual volume: 33.9% imports unclassified — visibility and documentation quality risk.
  • ⚠Moderate visible value: $3.77M + $2.55M — service revenue upside depends on consolidation and ILM evolution.
  • ⚠Port/customs fragmentation: 6 active hubs create coordination complexity for Akzent as well.
  • ⚠Urgency pressure: Air dependency may indicate reactive firefighting rather than planned logistics — manage expectations.
08 Commercial Opportunity Map
Customs Brokerage MUY ALTA IMMEX regimes, virtual transfers, regularization and multiple customs points
Air Freight / Expedites MUY ALTA 101 import + 22 export air shipments — OEM production continuity driver
Documentation Control MUY ALTA Industrial OEM operation with IMMEX sensitivity — zero tolerance for errors
Cross-Border (Colombia / Laredo) ALTA ~207 shipments through Colombia NL — Akzent's core corridor strength
Ocean Freight (Altamira) ALTA ~191 shipments via Altamira — primary maritime exposure on both import/export
Supplier Coordination (Spain + India) ALTA 230 combined shipments from Spain and India — multi-origin consolidation opportunity
Visibility ALTA 6 active hubs with no unified visibility — strong pain point for operational control
Warehousing / Staging MEDIA-ALTA Border staging at MTY / Laredo / Colombia — supports urgency management
ILM / 4PL ALTA (evolution) Integral coordination viable once entry through critical operation is established
09 Commercial Positioning

Position as operational continuity partner · customs + air urgency specialist · multimodal coordinator for OEM-dependent industrial supply chains.

"Vemos una operación industrial con alta mezcla de importación aérea, abastecimiento desde España e India, actividad marítima por Altamira y cruces recurrentes por Colombia / Nuevo Laredo. Normalmente este tipo de operación empieza a enfrentar presión en visibilidad, coordinación aduanal, urgencias y sincronización con cliente OEM. Queremos entender dónde están hoy los puntos de fricción y dónde Akzent puede apoyar con control operativo."
Customs / IMMEX Air Urgency Cross-Border Altamira Ocean Spain + India Sourcing ILM Evolution
10 Discovery Question Engine
Air Freight & Urgencies+
  • ¿Qué porcentaje de los embarques aéreos responde a urgencias o faltantes?
  • ¿Los aéreos están planeados desde el origen o son mayormente reactivos?
  • ¿Qué tan sensible es John Deere a retrasos o errores documentales en los aéreos?
Customs & IMMEX Operations+
  • ¿Los Shipments virtuales / IMMEX generan carga administrativa relevante?
  • ¿Con cuántos agentes aduanales trabajan actualmente entre Colombia, Altamira, MTY, AICM y Nuevo Laredo?
  • ¿Han tenido problemas de regularización o cambios de régimen en el último año?
Supplier Coordination (Spain & India)+
  • ¿La coordinación de España e India está centralizada o la maneja cada área de compras por separado?
  • ¿Tienen visibilidad del estatus de cada embarque desde origen hasta planta?
  • ¿Los proveedores de India generan más urgencias o problemas documentales que los de España?
Cross-Border & Hub Visibility+
  • ¿Tienen visibilidad unificada de Altamira, MTY, Colombia, AICM y Nuevo Laredo o cada hub se gestiona por separado?
  • ¿Hay necesidad de staging, buffer o consolidación en frontera para soportar las urgencias?
  • ¿Dónde tienen más fricción hoy: proveedor, aduana, transporte o cliente final?
11 Strategic Expansion Trajectory
Phase 1
Entry
  • Customs + IMMEX coordination
  • Air freight urgencies
  • Colombia NL / Nuevo Laredo
Phase 2
Integration
  • Altamira ocean management
  • Documentation control
  • Multimodal visibility
Phase 3
Coordination
  • Spain + India supplier sync
  • Warehousing / border staging
  • Unified hub intelligence
Phase 4
ILM / 4PL
  • Integrated OEM supply chain
  • Proactive urgency management
  • Full operational ownership
AA+ Classification Justification

AA+ for: real multimodal operation with 4 active modes, strong air urgency exposure (101 import + 22 export), OEM ecosystem dependency that elevates every service to critical tier, IMMEX/virtual complexity requiring expert customs brokerage, multi-origin sourcing from Spain and India, and ILM evolution pathway once entry established. Below AAA due to moderate visible value and absence of JIT automotive or pharma-grade regulatory complexity.

Active Client · Export / Air / Cross-Border / IMMEX · Consolidated SKX Account
SKX
Export Orchestration / Air Urgency / Cross-Border / IMMEX · Monterrey / Nuevo Laredo / AIFA
CLIENT AA
01 Account Intelligence Snapshot

SKX is an active Akzent CLIENT with strong early profitability but minimal penetration. All uploaded records — SKX, SKX S.A. de C.V., Skx S.A. De C.V., SK Express, SK Express Inc. and Sk Express Inc. — are consolidated into a single SKX account. Today Akzent captures only import Shipments, almost entirely terrestrial, with one maritime Shipment. Panjiva reveals a far larger visible universe than previously estimated: SKX moves 1,167 export Shipments alongside 442 import Shipments. Air coverage is zero against 1,080 visible air Shipments, and export coverage is zero against 1,167 visible export Shipments. This is not a satisfied account — it is a CLIENT AA expansion and wallet-penetration opportunity. The conversation must shift from transactional execution to operational control: export orchestration, Nuevo Laredo cross-border coordination, IMMEX visibility, customs control, air urgency and centralized Shipment visibility.

07 Operational Signals → Pain Mapping
Export Under-Capture (0% of 1,167)
HIGH probability
1,167 export Shipments visible in Panjiva, 0 captured by Akzent. Export orchestration to USA / Canada is the dominant whitespace and primary expansion lever.
Air Freight Whitespace (0% of 1,080)
HIGH probability
1,080 air Shipments (67.1% of the universe) with zero Akzent air capture. Air urgency and expedite control is a structural entry angle from Monterrey.
IMMEX / Return of Goods Complexity
HIGH probability
1,090 Return of Goods IMMEX + 89 Temporary IMMEX + virtual transfers = a 1,252-Shipment IMMEX-sensitive universe generating heavy customs coordination burden.
Nuevo Laredo Cross-Border Concentration
HIGH probability
320 import + 153 export Shipments through Nuevo Laredo make this a structural dependency. Any friction at this crossing impacts the entire operation.
Courier / Multi-Provider Fragmentation
MEDIUM-HIGH
328 Shipments routed through courier companies — control distributed across multiple providers with no unified visibility layer. Strong Control Tower fit.
Customer Coordination (OEM Network)
MEDIUM-HIGH
Concentrated export customers — Caterpillar, John Deere, Skyjack, Liebherr — elevate the cost of any delay or documentation error and demand reliable export coordination.
08a AIOS Green Flags
  • ✓Already inside the account: active CLIENT with $5,780 revenue, $1,996.54 GP and 34.5% margin in 2026 YTD.
  • ✓Large visible universe: 1,609 Shipments (442 imp / 1,167 exp) — substantial wallet headroom.
  • ✓Air-led operation: 1,080 air Shipments = immediate urgency / expedite entry opportunity.
  • ✓Cross-border + IMMEX exposure: Nuevo Laredo concentration — Akzent's core territory.
  • ✓Control Tower fit: multi-provider, courier-heavy, IMMEX-sensitive structure suited to an ILM / Control Tower layer.
08b AIOS Red Flags
  • ⚠Critical low coverage: 0.4% total — only 7 of 1,609 Shipments captured.
  • ⚠Export whitespace: 0 of 1,167 export Shipments captured — the dominant direction is uncaptured.
  • ⚠Air freight gap: 0 of 1,080 air Shipments captured — no visible air penetration.
  • ⚠High courier dependency: 328 courier Shipments = control fragmented across providers.
  • ⚠Low ocean activity: no maritime visible in uploaded files — limited modal diversification (1 Akzent maritime Shipment).
09 Commercial Opportunity Map
Export Orchestration MUY ALTA 1,167 export Shipments to USA / Canada, 0 captured — the dominant whitespace
Air Freight / Expedited MUY ALTA 1,080 air Shipments (67.1% of universe), 0 captured — urgency control entry angle
IMMEX / Returns Control MUY ALTA 1,090 Return of Goods IMMEX + 89 Temporary IMMEX — documentation & regime control
Cross-Border (Nuevo Laredo) MUY ALTA 320 import + 153 export Shipments through Nuevo Laredo — Akzent's core corridor
Customs / Regime Visibility MUY ALTA 1,252-Shipment IMMEX / virtual / regularization universe needing unified visibility
Control Tower MUY ALTA Multi-provider, courier-heavy structure across import, export, air and IMMEX
Supplier & Customer Coordination ALTA Concentrated OEM customers (Caterpillar, John Deere, Skyjack, Liebherr)
Warehousing / Border Staging MEDIA Border staging at Nuevo Laredo supports urgency and crossing management
Ocean Freight BAJA No meaningful ocean activity in uploaded files — Akzent has 1 captured maritime Shipment
10 Commercial Positioning

SKX already works with Akzent, yet Akzent captures only a small fraction of the visible universe. Panjiva reveals a far broader operation driven by exports, air Shipments, IMMEX returns and cross-border lanes. Position Akzent as a Control Tower and operational coordination partner across export execution, urgent air flows, Nuevo Laredo crossings, customs documentation and Shipment visibility — not as a transactional execution provider.

"Vemos una operación mucho más amplia de lo que refleja el negocio capturado hoy: exportaciones dominantes, fuerte actividad aérea, retornos IMMEX y cruces recurrentes por Nuevo Laredo y Monterrey. SKX ya trabaja con Akzent, pero la cobertura es muy baja frente al universo visible. La oportunidad es ayudar a SKX a centralizar el control de la ejecución de exportaciones, los flujos aéreos urgentes, los cruces fronterizos, la documentación aduanal y la visibilidad de cada Shipment. Queremos posicionarnos como una capa de Control Tower y coordinación operativa, no como un proveedor transaccional."
Export Orchestration Air Urgency IMMEX / Returns Cross-Border Customs / Visibility Control Tower Wallet Expansion
11 Discovery Question Engine
Operational Pressure & Control+
  • ¿Qué parte de SKX genera la mayor presión operativa hoy: ejecución de exportaciones, urgencia aérea, cruce por Nuevo Laredo, retornos IMMEX, documentación o visibilidad?
  • ¿Qué parte de la operación ya está controlada por Akzent versus distribuida entre otros proveedores?
Exports & IMMEX Returns+
  • ¿Por qué la actividad de exportación es tan grande frente a las importaciones, y cómo se coordina hoy esa red de exportación?
  • ¿Los Shipments de retorno IMMEX están centralizados o se gestionan entre varios proveedores, brokers y equipos internos?
Air Urgency & Delays+
  • ¿Qué porcentaje de los Shipments aéreos son urgencias reales versus flujo recurrente?
  • ¿Dónde ocurren más seguido los retrasos: origen, documentación, broker, cruce, carrier, aeropuerto, cliente o entrega final?
Visibility & Value+
  • ¿Existe un dashboard centralizado para ver cada Shipment por estatus, régimen, punto aduanal, modo, proveedor y destino?
  • ¿Qué generaría más valor para SKX: menos urgencias, mejor control de exportaciones, mejor control documental IMMEX, visibilidad, reducción de costo premium o continuidad operativa?
12 Strategic Expansion Trajectory
Phase 1
Terrestrial Base
  • Expand terrestrial imports
  • Nuevo Laredo crossing
  • Build on current capture
Phase 2
Export Support
  • Export execution
  • IMMEX returns
  • USA & Canada lanes
Phase 3
Air Urgency
  • Capture air expedites
  • Recurrent air from Monterrey
  • Urgency management
Phase 4
Customs / IMMEX
  • Documentation control
  • Import & export regimes
  • Compliance visibility
Phase 5
Control Tower
  • Centralized visibility
  • Providers, brokers, customers
  • Alerts & coordination
Phase 6
Wallet Expansion
  • From 0.4% coverage
  • Toward 5–10% target
  • vs Panjiva visible universe
AA Classification Justification

AA for: active CLIENT with a large, export-heavy visible universe (1,609 Shipments — 442 imp / 1,167 exp), air-dominant operation (1,080 air Shipments / 67.1%), strong cross-border, customs and IMMEX exposure (1,252-Shipment IMMEX-sensitive universe), high concentration in Nuevo Laredo and Monterrey, and a multi-provider structure structurally suited to a Control Tower. Coverage is only 0.4% (7 of 1,609) with 0% air and 0% export capture — the largest whitespace and highest-priority expansion lever in this account. Managed as a strategic expansion and wallet penetration target: convert a small import entry point into structured export, air and IMMEX coverage.

Active Client · Automotive / Plastics / Industrial Components · Strategic Expansion
Techniplas + DMP Monterrey
Automotive / Plastics / Industrial Components · Nuevo Laredo / Colombia NL / Altamira / Manzanillo
CLIENT AAA
01 Account Intelligence Snapshot

Techniplas + DMP Monterrey is an active Akzent client with strong 2025 contribution and improving margin in 2026, but directional coverage remains low versus a large, export-heavy visible Panjiva universe. The account should be managed as a strategic expansion client, with current strength in maritime and air imports and critical whitespace across exports, cross-border and the DMP Monterrey entity. This is an operationally complex multi-entity industrial operation with pressure around multimodal coordination, customs and visibility — Akzent should be positioned as an operational complexity and Control Tower partner, not as a transactional provider.

06 Operational Signals → Pain Mapping
Export Whitespace (1.1% capture)
HIGH probability
Akzent captures only 7 of 647 visible export shipments. An export-heavy ecosystem managed largely outside Akzent = the single largest expansion opportunity.
DMP Monterrey Entity Gap
HIGH probability
Critical whitespace across the DMP Monterrey entity. Multi-entity visibility and coordination is largely uncaptured and structurally suited to a Control Tower layer.
Cross-Border Coordination
HIGH probability
330 truck shipments plus 505 unknown/virtual shipments concentrated on Nuevo Laredo / Colombia NL = heavy cross-border exposure with fragmented visibility.
Air Freight Urgency
MEDIUM-HIGH
104 visible air shipments and active Impo Aéreo capture indicate urgency flows tied to production continuity — high-value, time-sensitive coordination.
Maritime Import Coverage
MEDIUM-HIGH
Maritime imports via Altamira / Manzanillo are Akzent's strongest current service but still partially covered versus the visible 83 maritime shipments.
Multi-Hub Fragmentation
MEDIUM-HIGH
7 active hubs (Nuevo Laredo, Colombia, Monterrey, Altamira, Manzanillo, MTY, Puebla) across two entities with no unified visibility layer.
07a AIOS Green Flags
  • ✓Active CLIENT base: 60 shipments in 2025 and 24 in 2026 YTD — confirmed, recurring Akzent relationship.
  • ✓Improving margin: 22.4% → 24.8% YTD — healthier mix and pricing discipline.
  • ✓Large visible universe: 1,024 shipments (377 imp / 647 exp) — substantial wallet headroom.
  • ✓Real multimodality: Truck, air, maritime and rail active — multimodal coordination value.
  • ✓Multi-entity structure: Techniplas + DMP Monterrey — structural fit for ILM / Control Tower.
  • ✓Cross-border concentration: Nuevo Laredo / Colombia corridor aligns with Akzent's core strength.
07b AIOS Red Flags
  • ⚠Export under-capture: Only 7 of 647 export shipments — 1.1% penetration on the dominant direction.
  • ⚠DMP Monterrey whitespace: Critical entity gap — largely outside current Akzent coverage.
  • ⚠High unknown/virtual volume: 505 shipments (49.3%) unclassified — visibility and documentation risk.
  • ⚠Low overall coverage: ~9.4% directional 2026 YTD — wallet expansion depends on cross-border and export entry.
  • ⚠Hub fragmentation: 7 active hubs across 2 entities create coordination complexity without a unified layer.

09 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as operational complexity partner · multimodal coordination partner · customs & visibility support · ILM / Control Tower partner · wallet expansion partner across Techniplas + DMP Monterrey — not as a transactional freight provider.

"Vemos una operación industrial multi-entidad (Techniplas + DMP Monterrey) con universo visible amplio y fuerte componente de exportación y cruce fronterizo por Nuevo Laredo y Colombia. Hoy Akzent participa principalmente en importación marítima y aérea, pero la mayor parte de la exportación, el cruce fronterizo y la entidad DMP Monterrey siguen sin cobertura. Queremos mapear la operación completa y proponer una capa de coordinación, visibilidad y control aduanal (ILM / Control Tower) que ordene la complejidad y expanda la cobertura."
Operational Complexity Multimodal Coordination Customs / Visibility Cross-Border ILM / Control Tower Wallet Expansion
10 Discovery Question Engine
Export & Cross-Border Coverage+
  • ¿Quién coordina hoy la exportación y los cruces por Nuevo Laredo y Colombia?
  • ¿Tienen visibilidad unificada del flujo de exportación o se gestiona por separado?
  • ¿Dónde tienen más fricción en el cruce fronterizo: aduana, transporte o documentación?
DMP Monterrey & Multi-Entity+
  • ¿Cómo se coordina hoy la operación entre Techniplas y DMP Monterrey?
  • ¿Existe una capa única de visibilidad y control entre ambas entidades?
  • ¿Qué proveedor logístico cubre actualmente la operación de DMP Monterrey?
Air Urgency & Maritime Imports+
  • ¿Qué porcentaje de los aéreos responde a urgencias de producción?
  • ¿Los marítimos por Altamira y Manzanillo están planeados o son reactivos?
  • ¿Qué tan sensible es la operación a retrasos o errores documentales?
ILM / Control Tower+
  • ¿Tendría valor una capa única de visibilidad y coordinación entre entidades?
  • ¿Cómo gestionan hoy las excepciones y desviaciones operativas?
  • ¿Quién consolida hoy la coordinación aduanal entre los distintos puntos?

AAA Classification Justification

AAA — Strategic Expansion Client for: confirmed active relationship with strong 2025 contribution and improving margin, a large export-heavy visible universe (1,024 shipments) with critical export and DMP Monterrey whitespace, heavy cross-border exposure on Akzent's core corridor, real multimodality, and a multi-entity structure that is structurally suited to an ILM / Control Tower layer. The expansion thesis is wallet and entity penetration, not new acquisition.

Cuenta Objetivo · Análisis Pre-Comercial · Ecosistema Farmacéutico
Sanfer Group
Manufactura Farmacéutica · Biotech · Packaging · Distribución LATAM · 6 entidades operativas
AAA
01 Resumen Ejecutivo del Ecosistema

El Grupo Sanfer no es una empresa farmacéutica simple — es un ecosistema operativo integrado de 6 entidades especializadas con una cadena de suministro bidireccional de alta complejidad. Importa insumos regulados, principios activos, materiales de empaque y reactivos biotecnológicos desde 12+ países; fabrica en México; y distribuye medicamentos, snacks y productos de consumo a toda LATAM. La entidad central de importación es Grimann (~$34.5M en importaciones), que abastece al grupo con medicamentos terminados y principios activos principalmente desde Europa vía aéreo. Probiomed opera una planta de biotech de alta complejidad con insumos de biorreactor desde USA/Suecia. La exposición regulatoria, la dependencia de proveedores especializados únicos y la distribución LATAM activa hacen de este grupo uno de los perfiles de mayor fit estratégico en el portafolio Akzent.

⚡ Señal Crítica de Oportunidad

Grimann importa $28.6M en air freight de un portafolio de medicamentos de 12+ proveedores europeos y americanos. Menarini (~$10.7M), Servier (~$6.9M) y Medac (~$5.0M) dominan. Esta concentración de air freight regulado es la mayor oportunidad comercial inmediata del grupo — y muy probablemente no está bien coordinada ni centralizada.

Insight: El air freight es el modo dominante por valor. Grimann solo ($28.6M air) supera el valor marítimo de todo el grupo. La coordinación de air farmacéutico regulado es la mayor oportunidad comercial.

06 Hub Intelligence — Puntos Aduanales del Ecosistema
HubDirecciónEmbarquesEntidadesSeñal Operativa
AICM — Aeropuerto CDMX Import 90+ Grimann · Dinafarma · Sanfer Hub aéreo primario del grupo. 90 importaciones Grimann solo. Mayor concentración de riesgo operativo único.
AIFA — Felipe Ángeles Import + Export 31 Grimann · Sanfer · Probiomed Hub aéreo secundario creciente. Sanfer usa AIFA para distribución LATAM (20 shipments).
Veracruz Import 58 Litogrimann · Grimann · Dinafarma Puerto marítimo principal. Litogrimann: 40 shipments packaging Suecia. Grimann: 16 medicamentos marítimo.
Ciudad Hidalgo, Chiapas Export 36 Aerobal · Litogrimann Corredor sur terrestre. Aerobal: distribución Walmart CA. Litogrimann: cajas cartón Costa Rica.
Manzanillo Export 37 Aerobal Distribución marítima Centroamérica. Aerobal: detergente + Microdacyn a Costa Rica/Perú.
Nuevo Laredo Export + Import 21 Grimann · Sanfer · Dinafarma Corredor USA. Sanfer: 6 exports. Grimann: 14 imports (Blistex Kank-A, Tecoland).
Colombia NL Import 9 Grimann Medicamentos Pharmetique Colombia. Corredor frontera norte secundario.
07 Mapa de Distribución LATAM — Exportaciones del Grupo
PaísEmbarquesEntidad(es)ProductoCorredor
🇨🇷 Costa Rica 35 Aerobal (26) · Litogrimann (9) Detergente · Cajas cartón farmacéutico Manzanillo Cd. Hidalgo
🇵🇦 Panamá 17 Sanfer (16) · Aerobal (1) Medicamentos (Sanfer CA y Caribe S.A.) AIFA Aéreo
🇬🇹 Guatemala 20 Probiomed (6) · Aerobal (14) Bioyetin · Filatil · Detergente (Operadora Tiendas) Aéreo Cd. Hidalgo
🇭🇳 Honduras 13 Probiomed (5) · Aerobal (7) · Sanfer (1) Biológicos · Microdacyn · Detergente Aéreo Cd. Hidalgo

🇺🇸 USA · 🇪🇨 Ecuador · 🇧🇴 Bolivia · Otros 22 Sanfer · Probiomed Medicamentos · Biológicos (Filgrastim) Aéreo Laredo
Insight Distribución: La distribución farmacéutica LATAM (Sanfer + Probiomed) opera 100% aéreo con Panamá como hub regional (Sanfer Centroamérica y Caribe S.A.). Aerobal usa corredores terrestres/marítimos para productos de consumo (detergente, Microdacyn antiséptico) a cadenas de supermercados Walmart-affiliated en Centroamérica.
08 Señales Operativas → Mapeo de Dolor del Ecosistema
Concentración Air Freight Regulado — Grimann
CRÍTICO · $28.6M
103 de 146 importaciones Grimann son aéreas. A. Menarini domina con 39 envíos. Alta probabilidad de fragmentación de agente aduanal y falta de coordinación central entre proveedores farmacéuticos europeos.
Insumos Biotech de Proveedor Único — Probiomed
ALTA probabilidad
Life Technologies + Global Life Sciences (ambos Thermo Fisher) proveen medios de cultivo críticos para biorreactor. Proveedor único de facto con 24 de 34 importaciones. Ruptura = paro de línea biológica.
Dual Hub Aéreo AICM + AIFA — Fragmentación
ALTA probabilidad
Grimann usa AICM (90 imports) y AIFA (11 imports). Sanfer usa AIFA (20 exports). Gestión split entre dos aeropuertos de CDMX implica doble agencia, doble coordinación, visibilidad parcial.
Packaging Farmacéutico Proveedor Único — Litogrimann
ALTA probabilidad
Intermat Trading (Suecia) provee 40 de 42 importaciones de cartón en bobinas vía Veracruz. Proveedor único de packaging crítico con cadena larga Suecia→Veracruz = vulnerabilidad operativa alta.
Distribución LATAM Farmacéutica 100% Aérea
MEDIA-ALTA
Sanfer exporta 27 de 34 por aire (Panamá hub). Probiomed 22 de 27 aéreo. Coordinación de exportaciones reguladas sin visibilidad cruzada entre entidades = doble gestión innecesaria.
Exportación Biológicos — Múltiples Países Simultáneos
MEDIA-ALTA
Probiomed exporta Filgrastim y Somatropina a Guatemala, Honduras, El Salvador, Bolivia, Pakistán, Alemania y USA. Requerimientos regulatorios distintos por país = alta carga documental por embarque.
09 Exposición de Riesgo Farmacéutico — Análisis por Entidad
Grimann — Riesgo Air Regulado
CRÍTICO
Probiomed — Riesgo Insumo Biotech
MUY ALTO
Litogrimann — Riesgo Packaging Único
ALTO
Sanfer — Riesgo Distribución LATAM
ALTO
Dinafarma — Riesgo API Regulado
ALTO
Aerobal — Riesgo Corredor Sur
MEDIO
10 Puntuación de Ajuste Akzent — Por Servicio
Air Freight Regulado (Grimann)
MUY ALTO
Aduana Especializada Farma
MUY ALTO
Coordinación Proveedor Único (Biotech)
MUY ALTO
ILM / 4PL Ecosistema Grupo
CRÍTICO
Ocean (Litogrimann / Aerobal)
ALTO
Distribución LATAM Export
ALTO
Justificación Clasificación AAA

AAA por: ecosistema multi-entidad con complejidad operativa cruzada, $28.6M en air freight regulado concentrado en Grimann, dependencias de proveedor único en tres entidades (Menarini/Grimann, Life Tech/Probiomed, Intermat/Litogrimann), operación biotech de alta especialización, distribución LATAM farmacéutica activa, y potencial ILM de nivel ecosistema que no existe en cuentas de entidad única.

11 Posicionamiento Comercial
Ángulo de Entrada

Entrar por Grimann — coordinación de air freight farmacéutico regulado. Con $28.6M en importaciones aéreas de 12+ proveedores europeos, la probabilidad de fragmentación de agencia, inconsistencia documental y falta de visibilidad centralizada es muy alta. Ese es el dolor más concreto y el mayor en valor.

"Un ecosistema que importa medicamentos regulados desde Alemania, Francia, Irlanda e India simultáneamente — vía 12+ proveedores distintos y dos aeropuertos — normalmente opera con visibilidad fragmentada, múltiples agentes aduanales y coordinación reactiva. Akzent puede centralizar esa capa operacional."
Air Regulado Aduana Farma Coordinación Biotech ILM Ecosistema Ocean Veracruz LATAM Export
✓ Green Flags
  • 🟢$34.5M en importaciones Grimann — mayor cuenta de importación del portafolio Akzent
  • 🟢103 air shipments anuales regulados — coordinación compleja garantizada
  • 🟢6 entidades = 6 puntos de entrada independientes al ecosistema
  • 🟢Probiomed biotech = nicho de alta especialización y stickiness muy alta
  • 🟢Litogrimann packaging = corredor marítimo recurrente y predecible
  • 🟢Potencial ILM de ecosistema completo = account más grande potencial del portafolio
⚠ Consideraciones
  • 🔴Requiere especialización en regulación sanitaria / COFEPRIS para diferenciarse
  • 🔴Ciclo de venta largo — farma no cambia de proveedor sin auditoría previa
  • 🟡Grimann puede tener agente aduanal dedicado consolidado — validar antes
  • 🟡Probiomed biotech requiere cadena de frío y manejo especializado
12 Motor de Preguntas de Descubrimiento
Grimann — Air Freight Regulado (Entrada Principal) +
  • ¿Cómo está estructurada actualmente la coordinación de importaciones aéreas farmacéuticas — tienen un agente aduanal único para todos los proveedores o trabajan con múltiples?
  • ¿El seguimiento de importaciones desde Menarini, Servier y los otros proveedores europeos está centralizado en un equipo o cada proveedor tiene su propio coordinador interno?
  • ¿Tienen visibilidad en tiempo real de todos los embarques aéreos activos simultáneamente?
  • ¿Cómo manejan las diferencias de timing entre AICM y AIFA para shipments del mismo proveedor?
Probiomed — Insumos Biotech y Biorreactor +
  • ¿Los medios de cultivo y resinas de cromatografía de Life Technologies tienen un lead time predecible o hay variabilidad que genera presión de inventario?
  • ¿Han tenido disrupciones en el abasto de medios de cultivo que hayan impactado la producción biológica?
  • ¿Qué pasa operativamente cuando un vial Schott llega con daño o defecto — tienen proceso de urgencia para reposición desde India?
  • ¿El manejo de temperatura para los medios de cultivo y biológicos está certificado en todos los puntos de la cadena?
Litogrimann — Packaging y Cadena de Cartón +
  • ¿El cartón de Suecia llega bajo un programa de entregas predefinido o es pedido contra demanda?
  • ¿Veracruz es el único punto de entrada o han considerado diversificar el puerto de recepción?
  • ¿Tienen contingencia si Intermat no puede entregar en tiempo — hay un proveedor alternativo de cartón calificado?
Coordinación de Ecosistema — ILM Oportunidad +
  • ¿Existe coordinación logística centralizada entre las entidades del grupo o cada empresa maneja su supply chain de forma independiente?
  • ¿Han evaluado consolidar la coordinación de proveedores europeos (Menarini, Servier, Intermat) bajo un solo operador logístico?
  • ¿Hay visibilidad cruzada entre Grimann, Probiomed y Litogrimann sobre embarques activos o cada área opera en silo?
13 Trayectoria de Expansión Estratégica
Fase 1
Entrada Grimann
  • Air pharma regulado
  • Menarini / Servier
  • Coordinación AICM + AIFA
  • Agente aduanal integrado
Fase 2
Probiomed + Litogrimann
  • Biotech supply (Life Tech)
  • Packaging Suecia → Veracruz
  • Coordinación de contingencia
Fase 3
Distribución LATAM
  • Export pharma Sanfer
  • Biológicos Probiomed
  • Aerobal corredor sur
Fase 4
ILM Ecosistema
  • Coordinación 6 entidades
  • Visibilidad centralizada grupo
  • Ownership operacional pleno
Cuenta Objetivo · Análisis Pre-Comercial · Inteligencia Panjiva
Saint-Gobain México
Materiales de Construcción · Vidrio · Yeso · Abrasivos · Soluciones de Alto Rendimiento · Operación Multimodal Import/Export
LEAD AAA
LEAD Relación: LEAD pre-comercial. Cuenta no operada por Akzent. Todo el análisis se construye exclusivamente sobre Panjiva Import & Export como fuente de verdad. La clasificación AIOS AAA indica fit estratégico y complejidad operativa — es independiente del badge de relación LEAD.
01 Resumen Ejecutivo — Inteligencia Panjiva

Saint-Gobain México opera una de las cadenas de suministro bidireccionales y multimodales más complejas visibles en el portafolio de análisis. Los registros Panjiva muestran una operación de importación masiva — 18,338 embarques visibles alimentando manufactura local desde China, USA, Europa y Asia — combinada con una plataforma de exportación regional de 4,990 embarques que distribuye a Norteamérica y toda Centroamérica. La operación cruza simultáneamente cinco modos de transporte (terrestre, marítimo, aéreo, ferroviario y double-stack rail) y más de una decena de puntos aduanales activos. Esta combinación de escala, dispersión geográfica de proveedores y coordinación multimodal define un perfil de alta complejidad operativa donde la visibilidad consolidada y la coordinación de control tower son el dolor estructural más probable.

⚡ Señal de Complejidad — Fuente: Panjiva

USD 799.3M de valor comercial visible analizado, repartido entre importación multimodal de 8+ países de origen y exportación a 8+ destinos. La fragmentación entre modos (60.7% de las importaciones bajo modo "Unknown" en los registros) y la dispersión entre Reynosa, Nuevo Laredo, Veracruz, AICM y AIFA sugiere coordinación distribuida sin una capa única de visibilidad — el espacio natural para un partner de ILM / Control Tower.

Import Shipments Analizados
10,000
de 18,338 visibles · Panjiva
Export Shipments
4,990
de 4,990 · cobertura completa
Valor Importado Analizado
$188.1M
USD · embarques de importación
Valor Exportado
$611.1M
USD · embarques de exportación
Valor Comercial Total Visible Analizado
USD 799.3M
14,990 shipments analizados entre importación y exportación · 5 modos de transporte · 8+ orígenes de importación · 8+ destinos de exportación. Datos derivados de Panjiva Import & Export.
02 Mix Modal — Importación
10,000 import shipments analizados
Unknown ❔6,068 · 60.7%
Terrestre 🛣1,362 · 13.6%
Aéreo ✈1,361 · 13.6%
Marítimo 🚢850 · 8.5%
Ferroviario 🚆358 · 3.6%
Insight: El 60.7% de las importaciones figura sin modo clasificado en Panjiva. La presencia simultánea de terrestre, aéreo, marítimo y ferroviario en proporciones relevantes confirma una operación genuinamente multimodal — difícil de coordinar sin una capa de visibilidad unificada.
03 Mix Modal — Exportación
4,990 export shipments
Terrestre 🛣3,350 · 67.1%
Marítimo 🚢1,064 · 21.3%
Aéreo ✈287 · 5.8%
Unknown ❔146 · 2.9%
Double Stack Rail 🚆141 · 2.8%
Insight: La exportación es terrestre-dominante (67.1%) hacia USA/Canadá, con un componente marítimo relevante hacia Centroamérica y Europa. El double-stack rail añade complejidad de coordinación intermodal poco común.
04 Orígenes de Importación — Red de Proveedores
🇨🇳 ChinaOrigen principal
🇺🇸 Estados UnidosCross-border intensivo
🇲🇽 MéxicoIntragrupo / doméstico
🇩🇪 AlemaniaEquipo / insumos técnicos
🇸🇰 EslovaquiaEuropa central
🇫🇷 FranciaCasa matriz / tecnología
🇯🇵 JapónComponentes especializados
🇮🇹 ItaliaInsumos industriales
05 Destinos de Exportación — Distribución Regional
🇺🇸 Estados UnidosDestino dominante
🇨🇦 CanadáCorredor norte
🇬🇹 GuatemalaHub Centroamérica
🇭🇳 HondurasCorredor sur
🇸🇻 El SalvadorCentroamérica
🇨🇷 Costa RicaCentroamérica
🇩🇪 AlemaniaReexport intragrupo
🇧🇪 BélgicaEuropa
06 Hub Intelligence — Puntos Aduanales de Importación
Punto Aduanal / PuertoDirecciónPerfil ModalSeñal Operativa
Reynosa Import Terrestre Punto de entrada cross-border principal. Alta concentración de import terrestre desde USA — corredor crítico de coordinación.
Nuevo Laredo Import + Export Terrestre Corredor Laredo — territorio operativo de mayor densidad. Cruza importación y exportación simultáneamente.
AICM — Aeropuerto CDMX Import Aéreo Hub aéreo primario. Insumos urgentes / alto valor desde Europa y Asia. Coordinación time-critical.
Veracruz Import Marítimo Puerto marítimo principal del Golfo. Volumen contenedorizado desde Europa y Asia.
AIFA — Felipe Ángeles Import Aéreo Segundo hub aéreo. Dual-airport CDMX (AICM + AIFA) implica coordinación dividida y visibilidad parcial.
Manzanillo Import Marítimo Puerto Pacífico para flujo asiático (China). Lead times largos y planeación de inventario.
Ciudad Juárez Import Terrestre Tercer corredor fronterizo terrestre. Cross-border secundario desde USA.
MTY Airport — Monterrey Import Aéreo Hub aéreo industrial del norte. Insumos para manufactura regional.
07 Hub Intelligence — Puntos Aduanales de Exportación
Punto Aduanal / PuertoDirecciónPerfil ModalSeñal Operativa
Nuevo Laredo Export Terrestre Salida principal hacia USA/Canadá. El corredor terrestre dominante de exportación (67.1% modal).
Veracruz Export Marítimo Salida marítima hacia Europa y Centroamérica (Bélgica, Alemania, Costa Rica).
Ciudad Hidalgo, Chiapas Export Terrestre Corredor sur hacia Centroamérica — Guatemala, Honduras, El Salvador. Distribución regional terrestre.
Reynosa Export Terrestre Segunda salida cross-border norte. Cruza con flujo de importación — doble dirección en mismo punto.
Colombia NL Export Terrestre Cruce frontera norte (Nuevo León). Territorio operativo Akzent de alta densidad.
Piedras Negras Export Terrestre · Rail Cruce ferroviario / terrestre. Punto natural para double-stack rail hacia USA.
AICM — Aeropuerto CDMX Export Aéreo Exportación aérea de alto valor / urgencia. Componente menor pero time-critical.
Altamira Export Marítimo Segundo puerto del Golfo. Salida marítima complementaria a Veracruz.
08 Señales Operativas → Mapeo de Complejidad
Operación Genuinamente Multimodal — 5 Modos Activos
ALTA probabilidad
Terrestre, marítimo, aéreo, ferroviario y double-stack rail operan simultáneamente en importación y exportación. Coordinar cinco modos sin una capa única de visibilidad genera handoffs reactivos y planeación fragmentada.
60.7% de Importaciones sin Modo Clasificado
ALTA probabilidad
6,068 de 10,000 import shipments figuran como "Unknown" en Panjiva. La falta de clasificación modal consistente sugiere data dispersa entre múltiples agentes y ausencia de una fuente única de verdad operativa.
Dual Hub Aéreo AICM + AIFA + MTY
ALTA probabilidad
Importación aérea repartida entre AICM, AIFA y Monterrey. Tres aeropuertos implican triple coordinación, múltiples agentes y visibilidad parcial sobre embarques aéreos activos.
Red de Proveedores Dispersa — 8+ Países
MEDIA-ALTA
China, USA, Alemania, Eslovaquia, Francia, Japón e Italia abastecen simultáneamente. Lead times heterogéneos y rutas mixtas (Pacífico vía Manzanillo, Golfo vía Veracruz, terrestre vía Reynosa) presionan la planeación de inventario.
Distribución Centroamérica Terrestre + Marítima
MEDIA-ALTA
Exportación a Guatemala, Honduras, El Salvador y Costa Rica vía corredor sur (Cd. Hidalgo) y marítimo (Manzanillo/Veracruz). Requerimientos documentales distintos por país elevan la carga operativa por embarque.
Cross-Border Bidireccional en Mismos Puntos
MEDIA-ALTA
Reynosa y Nuevo Laredo cruzan importación y exportación simultáneamente. Gestionar ambos sentidos en el mismo punto aduanal sin coordinación integrada multiplica la complejidad de documentación y timing.
09 Puntuación de Ajuste Akzent — Por Capacidad
Partner de Complejidad Operativa
CRÍTICO
Coordinación Multimodal (5 modos)
MUY ALTO
Soporte Aduanal & Visibilidad
MUY ALTO
ILM / Control Tower
CRÍTICO
Cross-Border Terrestre (Laredo/Reynosa)
ALTO
Distribución Export LATAM
ALTO
Justificación Clasificación AIOS AAA

AAA por: escala de operación (14,990 shipments analizados · USD 799.3M visibles), complejidad multimodal genuina con cinco modos activos, red de proveedores dispersa en 8+ países, operación bidireccional cross-border en los mismos puntos aduanales, distribución regional a Norteamérica y Centroamérica, y potencial de ILM / Control Tower de nivel corporativo. La clasificación AIOS es independiente del estatus de relación LEAD.

10 Posicionamiento Comercial
Ángulo de Entrada

Entrar como partner de complejidad operativa y coordinación multimodal. Una operación que cruza cinco modos, 8+ orígenes y una decena de puntos aduanales — con el 60.7% de las importaciones sin modo clasificado — opera casi con certeza con visibilidad fragmentada y coordinación distribuida. Akzent puede aportar la capa de control tower / ILM que consolida esa operación.

"Una operación que importa desde China, Europa y USA por aire, mar, tierra y ferrocarril simultáneamente — y exporta a Norteamérica y toda Centroamérica — normalmente opera con visibilidad parcial, múltiples agentes y coordinación reactiva entre modos. Akzent puede unificar esa capa operacional con soporte aduanal y un control tower multimodal."
Complejidad Operativa Coordinación Multimodal Soporte Aduanal Visibilidad Unificada ILM / Control Tower Cross-Border
✓ Green Flags
  • 🟢14,990 shipments analizados · USD 799.3M visibles — escala estructural de alta complejidad
  • 🟢Cinco modos de transporte activos — necesidad clara de coordinación multimodal
  • 🟢60.7% de importaciones sin modo clasificado — señal de visibilidad fragmentada
  • 🟢Triple hub aéreo (AICM · AIFA · MTY) — coordinación dividida que un control tower resuelve
  • 🟢Operación bidireccional en Reynosa y Nuevo Laredo — territorio Akzent de alta densidad
  • 🟢Distribución a 8+ destinos incl. Centroamérica — fit estructural de ILM regional
⚠ Consideraciones
  • 🔴Corporativo global — probable estructura de procurement y logística centralizada en casa matriz
  • 🔴Escala implica proveedores logísticos incumbentes ya establecidos — validar entrada por punta operativa
  • 🟡El modo "Unknown" debe validarse en discovery — confirmar si es gap de data o de coordinación
  • 🟡Múltiples entidades / plantas posibles — mapear quién decide logística por modo y región
11 Motor de Preguntas de Descubrimiento
Coordinación Multimodal (Entrada Principal) +
  • ¿Cómo está estructurada hoy la coordinación entre los distintos modos — terrestre, marítimo, aéreo y ferroviario tienen equipos / agentes separados o existe una coordinación integrada?
  • ¿Tienen visibilidad en tiempo real de todos los shipments activos simultáneamente, independientemente del modo y del punto de entrada?
  • ¿Quién consolida la información cuando un mismo flujo combina más de un modo de transporte?
  • ¿Cómo manejan los handoffs entre el tramo internacional y el tramo doméstico de cada shipment?
Importación & Red de Proveedores Global +
  • Con orígenes en China, Europa y USA simultáneamente, ¿cómo balancean lead times tan distintos en la planeación de inventario?
  • ¿La importación aérea se concentra en un solo aeropuerto o se reparte entre AICM, AIFA y Monterrey según urgencia?
  • ¿Tienen una fuente única de verdad sobre el estatus de cada importación o la información vive en distintos sistemas / agentes?
  • ¿Qué tan predecible es el tránsito marítimo desde Asia vía Manzanillo frente al flujo europeo vía Veracruz?
Exportación & Distribución Regional +
  • La exportación a Centroamérica combina corredor sur terrestre y salida marítima — ¿cómo deciden el modo por destino?
  • ¿Los requerimientos documentales distintos por país (Guatemala, Honduras, El Salvador, Costa Rica) generan carga operativa o retrabajos?
  • ¿El cruce bidireccional en Reynosa y Nuevo Laredo se gestiona de forma integrada o import y export operan en silos?
  • ¿Utilizan double-stack rail de forma programada o es una opción puntual según capacidad?
Visibilidad & Control Tower — ILM Oportunidad +
  • ¿Existe hoy una capa única de visibilidad que abarque importación, exportación y todos los modos, o cada área reporta por separado?
  • ¿Han evaluado consolidar la coordinación operativa bajo un único partner de control tower?
  • ¿Qué tanto de la operación se gestiona de forma reactiva por falta de información anticipada?
  • ¿Cómo medirían el valor de una capa de ILM que unifique la coordinación multimodal y el soporte aduanal?
12 Trayectoria de Engagement Propuesta
Fase 1
Entrada Operativa
  • Cross-border Laredo / Reynosa
  • Soporte aduanal puntual
  • Validar gap de visibilidad
  • Prueba en un corredor
Fase 2
Coordinación Multimodal
  • Integrar aéreo + marítimo
  • Unificar hubs CDMX + MTY
  • Visibilidad import consolidada
Fase 3
Distribución Export
  • Corredor sur Centroamérica
  • Salida marítima Europa
  • Documentación por país
Fase 4
ILM / Control Tower
  • Visibilidad unificada total
  • Coordinación 5 modos
  • Ownership operacional pleno
Active Client · Medical Manufacturing · IMMEX / Cross-Border Operation
Laerdal
Medical Manufacturing · Simulation & Training Products · Colombia NL / MTY Airport
CLIENT AAA
01 Account Intelligence Snapshot

Laerdal is an active Akzent client with real but low penetration. Akzent's current position is concentrated in truck and ocean imports — both underpenetrated relative to the visible Panjiva universe. Air freight coverage is zero. Export coverage is near zero. The IMMEX / customs / regime universe (653 Panjiva shipments) is entirely uncaptured. This is not a satisfied account — this is a CLIENT AAA expansion and wallet penetration opportunity. The conversation must shift from freight execution toward operational control: exports, air urgency, IMMEX visibility, customs coordination and cross-border orchestration.

Signal: 190 air imports into Monterrey Airport = urgent components and sensitive replenishment. All 190 uncaptured by Akzent.

Signal: 160 air exports — Laerdal sends critical medical equipment globally via air. Akzent captures 0.8% of all exports.

05 Critical Hub Intelligence
Hub / Customs Point Type Shipments Implication
Colombia, NLCross-Border~667 imp / ~228 expPrimary cross-border hub for IMMEX imports and outbound exports — Akzent core corridor
Monterrey AirportAir188 imp / ~194 expHigh-urgency air — critical medical components in, global product out — 0% Akzent capture
AltamiraMaritime33 impOcean freight support — Akzent has 29.5% penetration on ocean, opportunity to expand
Nuevo LaredoBorder27 impSecondary cross-border — additional truck and customs coordination potential
ManzanilloMaritime26 imp / 9 expAsia sourcing and minor ocean exports — longer-term ocean coordination opportunity
06 Operational Signals → Pain Mapping
Air Freight Urgency — Zero Akzent Capture
CRITICAL
350 Panjiva air shipments (190 imports + 160 exports) with 0 Akzent captures. Urgent medical components and global equipment dispatch controlled by a competitor. This is the single largest whitespace signal in the account.
IMMEX / Virtual Regime Complexity
HIGH probability
653 shipments in unknown/virtual/regime category — IMMEX temporary imports, returns, virtual transfers and regime changes. Heavy manual conciliation burden for a regulated medical manufacturer.
Export Near-Absence (0.8% coverage)
HIGH probability
474 Panjiva export shipments. Akzent captures only 4. Laerdal ships globally — medical simulation products to hospitals and training centers worldwide. This corridor is structurally controlled elsewhere.
Colombia NL Cross-Border Fragmentation
MEDIUM-HIGH
667 import + 228 export shipments through Colombia NL. Even with Akzent's foothold, the remaining uncaptured volume suggests fragmented carrier and customs agent relationships at the border.
Intracompany / Global HQ Forwarding Contracts
MEDIUM-HIGH
Many shipments are intracompany flows from Laerdal's global network. Air freight and export lanes may be governed by global forwarder contracts out of Norway HQ — requires internal champion to unlock.
ILM / Control Tower Gap
HIGH — AAA
1,446 Panjiva shipments across physical freight and regime/documentation universe with no unified visibility platform. Multi-agent, multi-carrier, multi-regime operation with no single coordination layer.
07a AIOS Green Flags
  • ✓Active client relationship: 77 captured shipments and $117K revenue — expansion starts from a trust base, not a cold pitch.
  • ✓High margin profile: 31.7% margin in comparable period — Laerdal is a high-quality, high-value revenue account.
  • ✓Large Panjiva universe: 1,446 shipments / $56.36M CIF — even 5% additional penetration represents meaningful revenue.
  • ✓Colombia NL foothold: Akzent has 15.4% truck penetration through its strongest cross-border corridor.
  • ✓Best ocean penetration rate: 29.5% on maritime — demonstrates capability on ocean that can be expanded.
  • ✓IMMEX complexity: Heavy regulatory / regime documentary operation creates a strong dependency opportunity for a specialized partner.
07b AIOS Red Flags
  • ⚠Air freight fully uncaptured: 350 Panjiva air shipments, 0 Akzent captures — another operator controls this lane entirely.
  • ⚠Export near-absence: 4 captures vs 474 Panjiva exports — export relationship does not exist at meaningful scale.
  • ⚠Global HQ contracts: Air and export lanes likely governed by Laerdal AS (Norway) global forwarding agreements — Akzent needs internal champion.
  • ⚠IMMEX compliance risk: Errors in IMMEX documentation create regulatory risk for a medical manufacturer — requires proven customs brokerage capability.
  • ⚠Low total penetration: 5.3% coverage — the account is a client in name but the wallet relationship is still fragile and underdeveloped.
08 Commercial Opportunity Map
Air Freight / Urgencias CRÍTICA 350 Panjiva air shipments, 0 Akzent captures — MTY Airport import + export air fully exposed
Export Capture MUY ALTA 474 export shipments, Akzent captures 4 — Colombia outbound, MTY air exports, export documentation
IMMEX / Customs Workflow MUY ALTA 653 regime/virtual shipments — temporary imports, returns, transfers, regularizations
Cross-Border Expansion (Truck) ALTA Truck penetration at 15.4% — Colombia NL and Nuevo Laredo have significant expansion room
ILM / Control Tower AAA (evolution) 1,446 shipment universe — unified visibility across freight, customs, regime, carrier and plant
Ocean (Altamira) MEDIA-ALTA 29.5% penetration already — expand from Altamira and Manzanillo existing base
09 Commercial Positioning

Position as existing operational partner · air urgency and export expansion specialist · IMMEX / customs coordination partner · cross-border orchestrator and ILM evolution candidate for a medical IMMEX operation.

"Laerdal ya confía en Akzent para parte de la operación terrestre y marítima de importación. La oportunidad real es expandir de ejecución a control operativo: exportaciones, urgencias aéreas, visibilidad IMMEX, coordinación aduanal y orquestación cross-border. Vemos un universo Panjiva de 1,446 embarques — con altísima exposición aérea, exportaciones relevantes y una capa documental compleja. Queremos entender dónde está la fricción hoy y cómo Akzent puede asumir más control de la operación."
Truck / Cross-Border Air Urgency Export Capture IMMEX / Customs Colombia NL Expansion ILM Evolution
10 Discovery Question Engine
Air Freight & Urgencias+
  • ¿La operación aérea está asignada a otro proveedor por especialización, urgencia o decisión de HQ?
  • ¿Qué porcentaje del aéreo es planificado versus reactivo?
  • ¿Se mide el costo de urgencias por causa raíz — o simplemente se ejecuta?
Exportaciones+
  • ¿Quién coordina hoy las exportaciones desde Colombia y Monterrey?
  • ¿Los flujos de exportación están centralizados o fragmentados por destino?
  • ¿Qué tendría que demostrar Akzent para capturar parte del wallet exportador?
IMMEX / Customs Operations+
  • ¿Cómo controlan transferencias virtuales, retornos IMMEX y cambios de régimen?
  • ¿Qué tan manual es la conciliación documental hoy?
  • ¿Dónde existe mayor riesgo: expediente, clasificación, tiempos o coordinación?
Cross-Border & Hub Visibility+
  • ¿Dónde se genera más fricción en Colombia / Nuevo Laredo: carrier, aduana, documentación o visibilidad?
  • ¿Tienen visibilidad unificada de cruces o cada punto se gestiona por separado?
  • ¿Cuáles son los KPIs críticos de frontera que hoy no se están midiendo bien?
11 Strategic Expansion Trajectory
Phase 1
Defend & Expand Base
  • Grow truck share Colombia NL
  • Consolidate ocean imports
  • Establish KPI reporting
Phase 2
Export Capture
  • Colombia outbound truck
  • MTY export documentation
  • Export compliance workflows
Phase 3
Air Urgency Entry
  • MTY Airport air imports
  • Export air flows
  • Root-cause urgency desk
Phase 4
ILM / Control Tower
  • IMMEX visibility workflow
  • Unified hub dashboard
  • Full operational ownership
AAA Classification Justification

AAA for: active client with real revenue and 31.7% margin, 1,446-shipment Panjiva universe with $56.36M CIF value, zero air freight capture on 350 Panjiva air shipments, 0.8% export coverage on 474 exports, extreme IMMEX / customs complexity (653 regime shipments), Colombia NL as structural hub where Akzent has operational strength, and AAA-grade ILM / control tower potential across the full operation.

Active Client · Food Manufacturing · Citrus Processing / Packaged Food
Del Monte
Industrias Citricolas de Montemorelos S.A. de C.V. · CLIENT Account · Panjiva Visible Universe + Akzent Captured Universe
CLIENT AAA
01 Account Intelligence Snapshot

Del Monte is a strategic CLIENT expansion account for Akzent. Panjiva shows 1,121 visible Shipments, including 857 export Shipments and 264 import Shipments. Akzent already captures 80 Shipments with USD 337,043.79 revenue and USD 56,375.73 Gross Profit, but the captured universe is almost entirely import terrestrial. This creates a clear wallet expansion opportunity across rail exports, truck exports, maritime coordination, customs control, supplier readiness and Control Tower / ILM. Akzent should not position only as a freight provider — Akzent should position as a logistics coordination and Control Tower partner.

06 Operational Signal to Pain Mapping
Export Concentration
USA Delivery Performance
High — 743 USA export Shipments via rail and truck. Customer delivery performance, border visibility and execution consistency are ongoing pressure points.
Rail Outbound
Post-Handoff Visibility
High — Rail is 76.2% of export volume. Visibility after rail handoff, customer update management and exception handling are structural gaps.
IMMEX Activity
Documentation Control Risk
Very High — 832 IMMEX return exports + 142 temporary imports. Documentation accuracy, regime control and customs coordination are high-frequency pain vectors.
Import Suppliers
Production Input Timing
High — Packaging and ingredient imports from USA, Thailand, China and Europe. Supplier readiness and production input timing create recurring coordination pressure.
Akzent Capture Gap
Relationship Underpenetrated
Very High — Relationship confirmed (80 Shipments, active seller) but capture is 100% import terrestrial. Export, maritime and rail represent confirmed whitespace.
Maritime Whitespace
Ocean Provider Gap
High — 161 maritime Shipments through Manzanillo and Altamira with 0% Akzent capture. Ocean coordination opportunity confirmed.
Rail Export Gap
Largest Service Whitespace
Very High — 653 rail export Shipments with 0 Akzent capture. Rail is the single largest gap in the account — highest-volume expansion opportunity.
Multi-Agent Customs
Fragmented Visibility
High — 6+ customs agents on import side. Fragmented visibility, inconsistent escalation logic and manual follow-up burden are high-probability operational friction.

AAA Justification

AAA classification driven by: 1,121-shipment visible universe with active CLIENT relationship at 7.1% coverage; rail-dominant export operation (653 rail Shipments, 0% captured) representing the single largest service whitespace in the account; IMMEX-heavy regime creating documentation coordination complexity; maritime whitespace via Manzanillo and Altamira; multi-agent customs footprint creating fragmented visibility; food / citrus manufacturing logistics continuity requirements; and confirmed Control Tower / ILM structural fit across inbound, outbound and customs layers.

08 AIOS Green Flags
  • 🟢Active CLIENT with 80 confirmed Shipments and USD 337K revenue — relationship is proven, not speculative
  • 🟢Import penetration at 29.5% — strong existing base on the import side to defend and expand
  • 🟢653 rail export Shipments with 0 Akzent coverage — largest single addressable whitespace in portfolio
  • 🟢IMMEX-heavy operation (832 return exports + 142 temp imports) — Akzent customs coordination positioning is structurally relevant
  • 🟢Monterrey-centric operation (649 export shipments) — home territory for Akzent with operational knowledge advantage
  • 🟢Multi-origin import base (USA, Thailand, China, Taiwan, EU) — supplier coordination complexity aligns with Akzent ILM capability
  • 🟢Reynosa and Nuevo Laredo in export footprint — Akzent border coordination territory
  • 🟢Maritime whitespace at Manzanillo and Altamira — ocean coordination opportunity with existing port knowledge
09 AIOS Red Flags
  • 🔴Rail export segment is 76.2% of export volume — if a competitor controls rail, displacement is structurally difficult
  • 🔴Export customs agent 3800 controls 655 export Shipments — single-agent dominance on export side may be entrenched
  • 🔴2026 YTD margin contraction to 13.3% vs 19.1% in 2025 — pricing pressure signal; needs validation before expansion pricing is set
  • 🟡Shipment volume dropped: 54 in 2024 → 16 in 2025 → 10 YTD 2026 — volume trend requires explanation before expansion planning
  • 🟡Food manufacturing logistics has phytosanitary and documentation requirements — Akzent must validate food-sector customs capability
  • 🟡Japan export lane (80 Shipments via maritime) — dedicated ocean provider likely already in place; entry requires a displacement story
10 Commercial Positioning
Strategic Positioning Angle

Del Monte already works with Akzent on terrestrial import execution, but Panjiva shows a much broader logistics universe: rail exports, truck exports, maritime Shipments, IMMEX activity, supplier inputs and customs complexity. The opportunity is to expand Akzent from execution provider into a Control Tower partner that gives Del Monte visibility, coordination and exception control across the full shipment lifecycle.

"Del Monte ya trabaja con Akzent en ejecución terrestre de importaciones — pero Panjiva muestra un universo logístico mucho mayor: 653 embarques de exportación ferroviaria, 161 marítimos, actividad IMMEX intensa y múltiples agentes de aduanas en importación. La oportunidad es escalar a Akzent de proveedor de transporte a Control Tower que centraliza visibilidad, coordinación y gestión de excepciones."
Control Tower Customs Coordination Rail Export Visibility Import Supplier Coord. Maritime — Manzanillo ILM Expansion
11 Discovery Question Engine
Current Akzent Lane Ownership +
  • Which Del Monte lanes does Akzent currently control?
  • Are the 80 captured Shipments recurring lanes or spot activity?
  • Which plants, suppliers or internal teams are tied to Akzent-managed Shipments?
  • What would justify giving Akzent more of the account?
Inbound Supplier Coordination +
  • Which packaging and ingredient suppliers create the most recurring shipment pressure?
  • Is supplier readiness tracked centrally?
  • How much visibility does the team have before cargo is ready?
  • Where do delays usually start: supplier, customs, border or carrier?
IMMEX / Customs Control +
  • How are temporary import and return documents controlled today?
  • Are customs issues tracked by broker, lane or root cause?
  • Which customs agents create the most friction?
  • Is there one owner for customs visibility or multiple points of contact?
Rail Export Visibility +
  • Who controls visibility after rail handoff?
  • How are USA customer updates managed?
  • What exceptions are most common in rail export Shipments?
  • Is rail performance reviewed by lane or customer?
Truck Export Execution +
  • Which USA-bound truck export Shipments are outside Akzent today?
  • How are Reynosa and Nuevo Laredo exceptions escalated?
  • Are export truck Shipments planned centrally or reactively?
  • Is there opportunity for Akzent to manage cross-border execution?
Maritime Whitespace +
  • Who controls Manzanillo and Altamira Shipments today?
  • Are ocean bookings centralized?
  • What causes port or documentation delays?
  • Could Akzent support maritime visibility and customs coordination?
Control Tower Readiness +
  • Would Del Monte benefit from one consolidated shipment visibility dashboard?
  • Are inbound and outbound exceptions reviewed together?
  • Could Akzent run a weekly or monthly logistics performance review?
  • What KPIs matter most: on-time performance, customs release, dwell time, cost, exception rate or customer service?
12 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity
Overall ILM Potential: Very High

Akzent should position as a Control Tower and operational coordination partner across inbound supplier Shipments, customs readiness, border execution, rail visibility, maritime coordination, exception management and food manufacturing logistics continuity.

Layer 1
Import Execution
  • Terrestrial import lanes
  • Supplier pickup visibility
  • Border coordination
  • Exception reporting cadence
Layer 2
Customs Control
  • IMMEX documentation layer
  • Customs agent coordination
  • Regime accuracy tracking
  • Centralized visibility
Layer 3
Export Expansion
  • Truck export coordination
  • Rail visibility layer
  • Maritime — Manzanillo
  • Export performance dashboard
Layer 4
Control Tower
  • Full inbound + outbound tracking
  • Supplier readiness dashboard
  • Exception management layer
  • Monthly KPI review
13 Strategic Expansion Trajectory
Phase 1
Relationship Mapping
  • Validate which lanes Akzent controls
  • Confirm recurring vs. spot activity
  • Map stakeholders: logistics, procurement, plant, customs, export CS
  • Identify who controls rail and maritime today
Phase 2
Tactical Expansion
  • Expand import terrestrial lanes
  • Target Reynosa / NL border coordination
  • Supplier pickup visibility
  • Build exception-reporting cadence
Phase 3
Export Penetration
  • Target truck export Shipments first
  • Build USA-bound visibility layer
  • Position on export docs + border coord.
  • Identify rail export visibility gaps
Phase 4
Control Tower / ILM
  • Centralize inbound + outbound tracking
  • Add supplier readiness dashboard
  • Add customs status dashboard
  • Monthly performance review with Del Monte
14 Final AIOS Verdict
AAA
CLIENT
EXPAND

Del Monte is a high-priority CLIENT expansion account. Akzent has confirmed captured business with 80 Shipments, USD 337K revenue and USD 56K Gross Profit. However, Panjiva shows a much larger visible universe of 1,121 Shipments, with major gaps in exports, rail, maritime and Control Tower visibility. Akzent already has the account — the opportunity is to expand from terrestrial import execution into full-account logistics control. The rail export gap (653 Shipments, 0% captured) is the single largest addressable whitespace. IMMEX complexity, multi-agent customs fragmentation and maritime whitespace at Manzanillo / Altamira are the supporting commercial entry points. Seller: Rogelio Berzoza.

07 Whitespace & Wallet Gap
Export Whitespace
VERY HIGH
Panjiva shows 92 export Shipments; Akzent captures 0. Entire export universe is open wallet — the single largest addressable gap.
Cross-Border / Truck Gap
VERY HIGH
Panjiva shows 70 truck Shipments; Akzent captures only 1 Terrestre Shipment. Cross-border execution to USA via Colombia / Nuevo Laredo is the largest service whitespace.
Air Freight Gap
HIGH
Panjiva shows 36 air Shipments; Akzent captures 16 import air and no export air. Room to grow air capture and improve margin quality.
ILM / Control Tower Opportunity
VERY HIGH
Multi-origin suppliers, import/export structure, air urgency and cross-border exposure create a strong control tower fit — supplier readiness, booking, visibility and exception management.
08 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

DOGA's structure — multi-origin suppliers (Spain, China, Italy, India, Brazil), a combined import/export flow, air urgency and recurring cross-border exposure to the USA — structurally demands a coordination layer above transactional freight. Akzent already holds the import base; the natural evolution is a centralized import/export control tower with weekly exception reporting.

Supplier Readiness
Origin booking sync across 5+ countries
Booking Coordination
Maritime + air consolidation control
Import / Export Visibility
Single layer across both flows
Exception Management
Structured escalation for urgencies
Monthly KPI Governance
Weekly exception + monthly review
09a AIOS Green Flags
  • ✓Strong import penetration: 80.6% import coverage — Akzent already owns the inbound base.
  • ✓Maritime import strength: 91.7% coverage — near-total control of ocean imports.
  • ✓Meaningful air capture: 66.7% air import coverage with clear room to grow.
  • ✓Revenue momentum: 2026 YTD ($161,162) materially above full-year 2025 ($53,754).
  • ✓OEM ecosystem: John Deere, Caterpillar, Bombardier — elevates every service to critical tier.
09b AIOS Red Flags
  • ⚠No export penetration: 92 visible export Shipments, 0 captured — largest wallet gap.
  • ⚠Cross-border / truck gap: 70 truck Shipments visible, only 1 Terrestre captured.
  • ⚠Margin compression: 32.2% (2025) → 20.9% (2026 YTD) — growth is quote-led, not value-led.
  • ⚠Multi-origin complexity: Spain, China, Italy, India, Brazil — coordination and documentation risk.
  • ⚠Air urgency exposure: 36 air Shipments may signal reactive firefighting vs planned logistics.
10 Commercial Opportunity Map
Export Execution (USA) MUY ALTA 92 export Shipments, 0 captured — entire export wallet is open
Cross-Border Control MUY ALTA 70 truck Shipments via Colombia / Nuevo Laredo — Akzent's core corridor
ILM / Control Tower MUY ALTA Multi-origin import/export structure with exception management need
Air Freight Management ALTA 36 air Shipments — grow capture and improve margin quality
Maritime Import (Protect) CORE 91.7% coverage — defend and deepen the existing base
Customs Coordination ALTA Multi-origin sourcing + export customs handoff complexity
11 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as DOGA's expansion partner for export execution, cross-border visibility, urgent air freight control and ILM / Control Tower coordination. Do not position Akzent as a transactional freight provider.

"We already manage the import base — maritime and air — at high coverage. The next step is helping DOGA control the export side to the USA, the cross-border execution through Colombia / Nuevo Laredo, the urgent air Shipments, and the visibility across suppliers, customs and final delivery through a single control tower with weekly exception reporting."
Export Execution Cross-Border Control Air Freight Management Customs Coordination ILM / Control Tower Exception Management
12 Discovery Question Engine
Export & Cross-Border Execution+
  • Which export lanes are currently most sensitive to delivery delays?
  • How are USA-bound Shipments coordinated today across carrier, customs and customer delivery?
Air Freight & Urgencies+
  • What usually triggers air freight for DOGA?
  • Are urgent Shipments handled as exceptions or through a structured escalation process?
Visibility & Control Tower+
  • How much visibility does DOGA have across supplier readiness, customs release and final delivery?
  • Which Shipments create the most internal follow-up workload?
  • Would DOGA benefit from a centralized import/export control tower with weekly exception reporting?
13 Recommended Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Protect & Grow Import Base
  • Maintain maritime import penetration
  • Improve air freight margin quality
  • Strengthen operational reporting
Phase 2
Export Pilot
  • Target USA export lanes via Colombia / Nuevo Laredo
  • Add export documentation & customs coordination
  • Start with recurring OEM / customer lanes
Phase 3
Cross-Border Control Desk
  • Carrier coordination
  • Border milestone visibility
  • Customs handoff escalation
  • Delivery confirmation
Phase 4
ILM / Control Tower
  • Supplier readiness & booking coordination
  • Import/export visibility
  • Exception management
  • Monthly KPI governance
AAA Classification Justification

AAA for: active CLIENT with confirmed captured business and strong import penetration, real multimodal operation (maritime, air, truck), a large uncaptured export universe (92 Shipments) concentrated on the USA cross-border corridor — Akzent's core territory — multi-origin OEM-grade sourcing complexity, and a structural ILM / Control Tower fit. Classified as an Expansion / Penetration Target: the relationship exists, the opportunity is to grow from import execution into full-account export and control-tower ownership.

Active Client · Industrial Energy · Technical Components / Instrumentation
Servicios Agregados de Energía
Industrial Energy / Technical Components / Instrumentation · MTY Airport / AICM / Manzanillo
CLIENT AA
01 Account Intelligence Snapshot

Servicios Agregados de Energía shows a specialized import operation with high technical cargo sensitivity, international supplier dependency, and strong multimodal complexity. Panjiva shows a visible universe heavily concentrated in air and ocean imports, while Akzent currently captures mainly terrestrial and ocean services. The main wallet gap is air freight: Panjiva shows 30 Air Shipments, while Akzent captured only 1 comparable Air Shipment in 2025–2026. Position Akzent as an operational coordination, customs visibility, air urgency, ocean execution, and ILM / Control Tower partner — not as a transactional freight provider.

05 Main Ports / Customs Intelligence
Hub / Customs Point Type Shipments Implication
MTY Airport / ApodacaAir19Primary air node — technical urgency and supplier-driven imports
AICMAir11Secondary air node — CDMX-area technical supply
ManzanilloMaritime11Principal ocean exposure — Asia-origin containers and equipment
Nuevo LaredoBorder1Complementary cross-border point — needs validation vs domestic capture
06 Operational Signals → Pain Mapping
Air Freight Urgency (71.4% of visible imports)
HIGH probability
30 visible air shipments vs only 1 captured by Akzent in 2025–2026. High technical-cargo air concentration via MTY Airport and AICM indicates reactive urgency and a clear wallet gap.
Technical Cargo Sensitivity
HIGH probability
Instrumentation, mass meters, sensors, pressure meters, control modules and electronic cards — zero-tolerance technical cargo where delays or documentation errors carry high cost.
Supplier Fragmentation
HIGH probability
10+ active suppliers across China, India, Turkey and the US. Multi-timezone, multi-currency coordination with documentation requirements that increase error probability.
Customs Exposure (multi-node)
MEDIUM-HIGH
Four customs points (MTY Airport, AICM, Manzanillo, Nuevo Laredo) with technical classification complexity — instrumentation and control components require careful tariff handling.
Ocean Coordination (Manzanillo)
MEDIUM-HIGH
11 maritime shipments via Manzanillo with Asia-origin equipment. Pre-alert and documentation control needed to confirm whether flows are recurrent or spot purchases.
Control Tower Gap
MEDIUM-HIGH
No unified visibility across air, ocean, customs and final delivery. Exception ownership between supplier, customs and transportation is unclear — strong ILM / Control Tower fit.
07a AIOS Green Flags
  • ✓Active client base: 37 captured shipments 2024–2026 with USD 59,608 revenue and 35.0% blended margin.
  • ✓Improving margin: 31.7% (2024) → 51.0% (2025) → 58.1% (2026 YTD) — strong profitability trajectory.
  • ✓Air whitespace: 30 visible air shipments vs 1 captured — immediate, well-defined expansion opportunity.
  • ✓Real multimodality: Air, maritime and terrestrial active simultaneously across the operation.
  • ✓Technical cargo value: Instrumentation and control components elevate every service to a critical tier.
  • ✓ILM / Control Tower fit: Multi-node customs and supplier fragmentation structurally demand a coordination layer.
07b AIOS Red Flags
  • ⚠Air coverage gap: Only 3.3% of visible air imports captured — wallet concentrated outside Akzent today.
  • ⚠Declining shipment count: 24 (2024) → 9 (2025) → 4 (2026 YTD) captured — defend the base before expanding.
  • ⚠Supplier fragmentation: 10+ suppliers across 4+ countries create documentation and compliance pressure.
  • ⚠Export blind spot: Panjiva exports not uploaded — export universe not quantifiable, needs validation.
  • ⚠Urgency pressure: High air concentration may indicate reactive firefighting rather than planned logistics.
08 Commercial Opportunity Map (Whitespace)
Air Freight Capture VERY HIGH 30 visible air shipments vs 1 captured — air urgency desk for MTY Airport and AICM
Customs / Documentation Control VERY HIGH Technical classification across MTY, AICM, Manzanillo and Nuevo Laredo — zero tolerance for errors
Ocean Freight (Manzanillo) MEDIUM-HIGH 11 visible vs 4 captured — ocean pre-alert and supplier coordination opportunity
Supplier Coordination (China + India) ALTA 10+ technical suppliers — multi-origin consolidation and documentation control
Visibility / Control Tower ALTA No unified visibility across air, ocean, customs and delivery — strong operational-control pain
Cross-Border Validation NEEDS VALIDATION Truck/terrestrial capture (8) not directly comparable to Panjiva (1) — confirm scope
ILM / 4PL ALTA (evolution) Supplier-to-door coordination with recurring review cadence once entry through air is established
09 Commercial Positioning

Position as continuity and operational control partner for critical technical imports — air urgency, ocean execution, customs visibility and ILM / Control Tower coordination. Do not position Akzent as a transactional freight provider.

"Your import profile shows high air and ocean dependency, technical suppliers, and several customs points. Akzent can help centralize visibility, anticipate exceptions, coordinate suppliers, and reduce operational friction across air, ocean, customs, and final delivery."
Air Urgency Desk Customs Visibility Manzanillo Ocean China + India Sourcing Control Tower ILM Evolution
10 Discovery Question Engine
Air Freight & Urgencies+
  • What type of components most frequently require air import?
  • Are air shipments planned, or do they respond to urgent operational needs?
  • Can Akzent review the top air shipments to detect recurring urgency patterns?
Suppliers & Documentation+
  • Which suppliers create the most documentation or compliance pressure?
  • Who currently owns exception management between supplier, customs, and transportation?
  • Where are most delays generated: supplier readiness, forwarder updates, customs, release, or final delivery?
Ocean & Manzanillo+
  • Are Manzanillo ocean imports recurrent or spot purchases?
  • Is there a need for pre-alert and documentation control on the ocean leg?
  • How are Asia-origin suppliers coordinated across ocean and air today?
Visibility & Control Tower+
  • Would a monthly dashboard of shipments, delays, modes, costs, and urgency root causes be useful?
  • Do you have unified visibility across MTY, AICM, Manzanillo and Nuevo Laredo, or is each managed separately?
  • Where do you have the most friction today: supplier, customs, transportation, or final delivery?
11 Strategic Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Defend Current Base
  • Review 2025–2026 captured shipments
  • Assess service profitability
  • Identify strongest repeat potential
Phase 2
Capture Air Wallet
  • Air urgency desk — MTY & AICM
  • Target technical imports
  • Supplier-driven urgency
Phase 3
Expand Ocean
  • Build Manzanillo visibility
  • Supplier coordination
  • Ocean pre-alert & doc control
Phase 4
Control Tower Light
  • Monthly KPI dashboard
  • By supplier, mode, customs, delay, cost
  • Centralize exception management
Phase 5
ILM
  • Supplier-to-door coordination
  • Recurring review cadence
  • Full operational ownership
AA Classification Justification

AA for: active client base with improving margin, real multimodal operation (air, ocean, terrestrial), very high air-urgency signal with a well-defined 30-vs-1 air whitespace, high technical-cargo sensitivity, supplier fragmentation across China / India / Turkey / US, and strong ILM / Control Tower fit. Below AAA due to smaller visible value (USD 1.26M CIF approx) and absence of JIT automotive or pharma-grade regulatory complexity, with declining captured shipment count requiring base defense before expansion.

07 Modal / Service / Export Whitespace
Export Whitespace
VERY HIGH
Panjiva shows 64 export Shipments; Akzent captures 6. The export universe around Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL is the single largest addressable gap.
Cross-Border Gap
HIGH
Cross-border activity concentrates on Nuevo Laredo (52) and Colombia NL (28). Recurring cross-border control is uncaptured beyond the current truck base.
Air Freight Gap
LIMITED
Panjiva shows 4 air Shipments; Akzent captures 2. Air exposure is small — not a primary lever for this account.
Ocean Opportunity
MEDIUM
Ocean is small but present (Panjiva 1, Akzent 5 captured incl. prior-period). A modest add-on alongside the core truck and export program.
08 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

Metalistik has a strong cross-border and export coordination opportunity. Akzent already holds a relevant captured truck base and should expand from truck execution into export coordination, customs visibility and recurring cross-border control around Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL.

09 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as the partner that can stabilize and expand Metalistik's cross-border and export execution, with visibility around Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL — not as a provider competing only for spot Shipments.

"We already move part of your truck base. The next step is helping Metalistik control the export side and the cross-border execution through Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL, with a single visibility layer across crossings, customs and delivery — turning reactive Shipments into a recurring, controlled program."
Export Coordination Cross-Border Control Customs / Visibility Multimodal Coordination ILM / Control Tower Wallet Expansion
10 Discovery Question Engine
Export & Cross-Border Execution+
  • Which export lanes create the most operational friction today?
  • Are Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL managed through one control process or separately?
  • How much of the export activity is planned versus reactive?
Current Akzent Participation+
  • Where does Akzent currently participate: specific lanes, specific suppliers, or spot decisions?
  • What would need to change for Akzent to manage a broader export / cross-border program?
Visibility & Control+
  • How much visibility does Metalistik have across customs release and final delivery?
  • Which Shipments create the most internal follow-up workload?
11 Recommended Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Protect Captured Truck Base
  • Maintain import truck penetration
  • Strengthen operational reporting
  • Defend margin gains
Phase 2
Expand Export Shipments
  • Target export lanes via Nuevo Laredo / Colombia NL
  • Add export documentation & customs coordination
  • Start with recurring lanes
Phase 3
Cross-Border Visibility
  • Formalize visibility for Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL
  • Border milestone tracking
  • Customs handoff escalation
Phase 4
Control Tower Scope
  • Recurring cross-border / export control
  • Exception management
  • Monthly KPI governance
AA Classification Justification

AA for: active CLIENT with a meaningful captured truck base and improving margin (27.1% → 34.2%), real cross-border exposure concentrated on Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL — Akzent's core corridor — and a clear export whitespace (64 visible vs 6 captured). Below AAA due to a smaller visible universe, limited air and ocean exposure, and the need to protect the captured base before broad expansion.

08 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

La Capital de Selladas is a strong candidate for supplier coordination, urgent import response, customs visibility and multimodal control. The high air volume suggests parts sensitivity, urgency and clear potential for better planning and escalation management across AIFA, MTY Airport, Nuevo Laredo, Manzanillo and Altamira.

Air Response Desk
Planned urgent import handling
Supplier Coordination
Origin readiness & booking sync
Customs Visibility
