09 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as the partner to reduce reactive urgent logistics by coordinating suppliers, documentation, customs and multimodal execution across AIFA, MTY Airport, Nuevo Laredo, Manzanillo and Altamira — converting reactive air Shipments into planned control.

"Your operation moves a large air volume through AIFA and MTY Airport, plus recurring cross-border through Nuevo Laredo. That pattern usually means urgency and reactive decisions. We want to help convert those urgent Shipments into a planned air-response model with supplier coordination, customs visibility and a single control layer."
Air Freight Response Supplier Coordination Cross-Border Control Customs / Visibility ILM / Control Tower Wallet Expansion
10 Discovery Question Engine
Air Freight & Urgency+
  • What usually triggers air Shipments: supplier delay, stockout risk, customer urgency, or production sensitivity?
  • Are air Shipments managed strategically or case by case?
Hubs & Operational Pressure+
  • Which hubs create the most operational pressure: AIFA, MTY Airport, Nuevo Laredo, Manzanillo, or Altamira?
  • How are urgent imports approved and controlled today?
Conversion to Planned Control+
  • Where can Akzent help convert reactive urgent Shipments into planned control?
  • What caused the 2026 YTD activity drop versus 2025?
11 Recommended Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Reconnect & Validate
  • Reconnect captured lanes
  • Validate the 2026 YTD drop
  • Stabilize the relationship
Phase 2
Air Freight Response Desk
  • Build a planned air response model
  • Supplier readiness & escalation
  • Target AIFA / MTY Airport
Phase 3
Cross-Border Coverage
  • Expand Nuevo Laredo cross-border
  • Customs handoff visibility
  • Border milestone tracking
Phase 4
Ocean & Supplier Coordination
  • Add Manzanillo / Altamira ocean
  • Multi-origin supplier sync
  • Consolidation control
Phase 5
ILM / Control Tower
  • Urgent import control layer
  • Visibility across hubs
  • Exception management
AA Classification Justification

AA / AAA Potential for: active CLIENT inside a large, air-heavy visible universe (502 Shipments) where Akzent captures only 5.18% — a very high wallet opportunity. Strong multimodal diversity, urgent air signal through AIFA / MTY Airport, cross-border through Nuevo Laredo and ocean through Manzanillo / Altamira create a structural ILM / Control Tower fit. Reaches AAA potential once the 2026 YTD activity is reconnected and an air-response program is established.

08 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

Euro Water Technologies is a strong candidate for a controlled recurring model because Akzent already has high coverage and improving margin. The opportunity is to convert a strong transactional base into structured import coordination across ocean, air and truck execution.

Recurring Import Control
Formalize a standing process
Ocean Defense

Margin Governance

09 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as the partner already embedded in the account, capable of formalizing a recurring import control model across ocean, air and truck execution — protecting the base while expanding margin.

"We already manage most of your import base through Altamira with strong, improving margins. The next step is making that participation recurring rather than shipment-by-shipment — a structured import control process across ocean, air and truck, with single-layer visibility from origin to plant."
Recurring Import Control Ocean Defense Air Capture Customs / Visibility ILM / Control Tower Margin Expansion
10 Discovery Question Engine
Recurring Participation+
  • What would make the current Akzent participation more recurring and less shipment-by-shipment?
  • Are Altamira imports planned centrally or managed case by case?
Remaining Capture+
  • Which Shipments still fall outside Akzent and why?
  • What causes air usage in this account?
Control Formalization+
  • Can Akzent formalize a control process for all import Shipments?
  • How is customs release coordinated across Altamira and MTY Airport today?
11 Recommended Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Defend Current Ocean Base
  • Protect Altamira ocean Shipments
  • Sustain margin gains
  • Strengthen reporting
Phase 2
Capture Remaining Air
  • Target remaining air imports
  • Improve air margin quality
  • MTY Airport coordination
Phase 3
Formalize Import Control
  • Recurring import process
  • Customs visibility layer
  • Origin-to-plant tracking
Phase 4
ILM / Control Tower (Light)
  • Convert base into a control model
  • Exception management
  • Monthly KPI governance
AA Classification Justification

AA for: active CLIENT with the highest Shipment penetration among the five (57.69%) and strongly improving margin (22.3% → 34.9%). Real multimodal import operation (ocean, air, truck) anchored on Altamira. The thesis is defense, retention and margin expansion rather than acquisition. Below AAA due to a small visible universe (26 Shipments), import-only structure and limited cross-border / air scale.

09 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as a specialized partner for critical technical imports, urgent air coordination and customs visibility through MTY Airport — able to protect margin while increasing coverage.

"Your operation is strongly air-dependent through MTY Airport, with technical components that are sensitive to delay. We want to become your default urgent-import desk — coordinating air arrivals, customs release and critical-parts visibility — while protecting the strong margins this account already runs."
Urgent Air Control Critical Parts Visibility Premium Response Customs / Visibility ILM / Control Tower Margin Protection
10 Discovery Question Engine
Air Dependence+
  • What drives the high number of air Shipments?
  • Are these planned imports or urgent replacements?
Activity Decline+
  • Why did 2026 YTD captured activity decline versus 2025?
  • Which technical components are most sensitive to delay?
Default Air Desk+
  • Can Akzent become the default urgent import desk for MTY Airport arrivals?
  • How is customs release coordinated for urgent air today?

Phase 1
Recover 2026 YTD Activity
  • Reconnect captured lanes
  • Rebuild the air import base
  • Stabilize the relationship
Phase 2
Capture Additional Air
  • Target remaining air imports
  • Protect premium margin
  • MTY Airport coordination

Premium Response Desk
  • Critical-parts response model
  • Urgent arrival handling
  • Escalation management
Phase 4

  • Monthly KPI governance
AA Classification Justification

AA for: active CLIENT with premium, stable margin (≈40.6%) and a strong air-dependence signal through MTY Airport (53 visible vs 7 captured) that fits Akzent's urgent-import and critical-parts profile. Below AAA due to a small visible universe (60 Shipments), import-only structure, limited cross-border exposure, and a sharp 2026 YTD activity decline that must be recovered before expansion.

08 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

Mekra Lang is the highest-priority CLIENT opportunity among these accounts. It has the strongest profile for Control Tower because of its high volume, balanced import/export structure, cross-border dependency, ocean exposure, air urgency and industrial / automotive operating profile. Akzent should help manage cross-border execution, export coordination, customs visibility, air escalation and ocean supplier coordination — not transactional freight.

Cross-Border Execution
Nuevo Laredo / Colombia NL control
Export Coordination
Attack the 1,818 export universe
Air Escalation
Urgent recovery support

Manzanillo supplier sync
Customs Visibility
Single layer across hubs
Entry Protection

09 Commercial Positioning

Position Akzent as an operational control partner that helps Mekra Lang manage cross-border execution, export coordination, customs visibility, air escalation and ocean supplier coordination. The message is not transactional — the goal is to convert a small 2026 entry point into a structured wallet expansion strategy.

"You run a very large, cross-border-heavy industrial operation through Nuevo Laredo, Colombia NL, Manzanillo and MTY Airport, balanced across import and export. We have just started with a few truck Shipments. We want to understand why Akzent was selected, protect that entry, and build a structured plan to expand into export, air and ocean under a single control layer."
Cross-Border Execution Export Coordination Air Escalation Ocean Coordination ILM / Control Tower Wallet Expansion
10 Discovery Question Engine
Entry Point+
  • What led Akzent to capture the first 7 Shipments in 2026?
  • Are those Shipments tied to a specific lane, plant, buyer, supplier, or escalation event?
Export & Friction+
  • Who controls export Shipments today?
  • Where does Mekra Lang experience the most friction: Nuevo Laredo, Colombia NL, Manzanillo, MTY Airport, or supplier coordination?
Air & Control Tower+
  • What percentage of air activity is urgent recovery versus planned premium freight?
  • Could Akzent pilot a Control Tower scope on one cross-border or export lane?
11 Recommended Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Protect 2026 Entry
  • Defend the first 7 Shipments
  • Understand why Akzent was selected
  • Stabilize the relationship
Phase 2
Expand Truck Cross-Border
  • Grow Nuevo Laredo / Colombia NL truck
  • Border milestone visibility
  • Customs handoff control
Phase 3
Attack Export Whitespace
  • Target the 1,818 export universe
  • Export documentation & customs
  • Start with recurring lanes
Phase 4
Air Escalation Support
  • Add urgent air recovery
  • MTY Airport coordination
  • Escalation management
Phase 5
Ocean Coordination
  • Add Manzanillo ocean
  • Supplier readiness & booking
  • Consolidation control
Phase 6
ILM / Control Tower Pilot
  • Cross-border + export + visibility
  • Single control layer
  • Monthly KPI governance
AAA Classification Justification

AAA for: active CLIENT with a very large visible operational universe (3,004 Shipments) balanced across imports and exports, extreme cross-border dependency on Nuevo Laredo and Colombia NL — Akzent's core territory — meaningful ocean and air exposure, and an industrial / automotive profile structurally suited to a Control Tower. Coverage is only 0.23%, making this the largest whitespace and highest-priority expansion opportunity in the set. Classified as the top expansion target: convert a small 2026 entry point into a structured wallet expansion strategy.

07 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

Position Akzent as an industrial import Control Tower partner with focus on supplier coordination, customs support, critical spare parts visibility, air urgency management and multimodal expansion from air into truck and maritime.

Supplier Coordination
Verder · Abel · Sundyne sync
Customs Support
Reynosa · Altamira · Nogales · MTY
Spare Parts Visibility
Part-/equipment-level tracking
Air Urgency Management
Formalize urgent air program
Multimodal Expansion
Air → truck & maritime
08 Commercial Positioning

Do not position Akzent as a transactional freight provider. Position Akzent as the operational control layer for industrial import reliability.

"We already handle your urgent air imports. The visible universe shows most of your pumps, valves and spare parts moving by truck through Reynosa and by sea through Altamira and Nogales. We want to bring those under one control layer — supplier coordination, customs, spare-parts visibility and a structured air urgency program — so industrial import reliability is protected."
Industrial Import Control Cross-Border (Reynosa) Maritime (Altamira / Nogales) Air Urgency Spare Parts Visibility ILM / Control Tower
09 Discovery Question Engine
Air Freight & Urgencies+
  • Were the Akzent air Shipments urgent spare parts or planned imports?
  • Which Shipments require part-number or equipment-level visibility?
Truck & Maritime Control+
  • Who controls the truck Shipments through Reynosa today?
  • Is the maritime flow through Altamira centralized or supplier-managed?
Inventory & ILM+
  • Is there critical inventory that could justify ILM or scheduled replenishment?
Active Client · Industrial / Medical Components / Technical Assemblies · Priority Expansion
Busman PI
Industrial / Medical Components / Plastic & Technical Assemblies · Nuevo Laredo / Guadalajara / Altamira
CLIENT AAA
01 Account Intelligence Snapshot

Busman PI is the strongest CLIENT account in this batch. Panjiva shows a bidirectional operation with imports of components, molds, plastic parts and technical inputs, and exports from Guadalajara tied to assemblies, plastic parts, electric components and likely medical training / mannequin-related goods. Akzent already captures 7 import Shipments, mainly maritime, with attractive profitability. The priority is to expand from current import capture into export, cross-border, customs, air urgency, supplier coordination and ILM / Control Tower.

08 ILM / Control Tower Opportunity

Position Busman PI as a priority expansion account. The opportunity is an import-export Control Tower covering supplier coordination, customs support, export documentation, visibility by order / component, and expansion from import maritime into export, cross-border and air urgency support.

Supplier Coordination
Laerdal · Hexpol · Rhetech sync
Export Documentation
Guadalajara export coordination
Order / Component Visibility
Single layer across both flows
Cross-Border Control
Nuevo Laredo truck expansion
Air Urgency Support
Molds / electric / technical parts
09 Commercial Positioning

Do not position Akzent as a transactional freight provider. Position Akzent as the operational coordination and visibility partner for a technical manufacturing supply chain.

"We already run your maritime imports with strong margin. The visible universe shows 14 truck Shipments through Nuevo Laredo and 17 exports from Guadalajara that we don't yet touch. We want to bring import and export under one control tower — supplier coordination, customs, export documentation and order-level visibility — and add cross-border and air urgency support."
Import Maritime (Protect) Export Execution Cross-Border (Nuevo Laredo) Customs & Documentation Air Urgency ILM / Control Tower
10 Discovery Question Engine
Supplier & Operating Flow+
  • Does the Laerdal relationship drive part of Busman PI's operating flow?
  • Are the Nuevo Laredo Shipments tied to recurring suppliers?
Export & Cross-Border+
  • Which export Shipments from Guadalajara are open to Akzent participation?
  • Does Akzent currently participate only in import maritime or also in customs / documentation?
Air Urgency+
  • Are there air urgency needs for molds, electric components or critical technical parts?
11 Recommended Expansion Roadmap
Phase 1
Protect Import Maritime
  • Defend the maritime import base
  • Recover air capture (molds / technical)
  • Strengthen operational reporting
Phase 2
Cross-Border Pilot
  • Target 14 truck Shipments via Nuevo Laredo
  • Supplier readiness & customs handoff
  • Start with recurring suppliers
Phase 3
Export Capture (Guadalajara)
  • 17 visible export Shipments
  • Export documentation control
  • Order / component visibility
Phase 4
ILM / Control Tower
  • Import + export single layer
  • Supplier coordination
  • Exception & air urgency management
  • Monthly KPI governance
AAA Classification Justification

AAA for: active CLIENT with confirmed captured business and the strongest profitability in this batch (20.2% margin), a bidirectional import/export operation (33 import + 17 export visible), real multimodal exposure (maritime, air, truck), a large uncaptured export universe (USD 2.14M) and 14 truck Shipments on the Nuevo Laredo corridor — Akzent's core territory — plus a technical / medical manufacturing profile structurally suited to a Control Tower. Classified as the priority expansion account: grow from import maritime capture into full-account export and control-tower ownership.
